svg.aria-label: The AI Agent Platform as built through Milestone 17. A GitHub webhook enters a Lambda Function URL that verifies the HMAC signature, filters the event, and publishes a curated event to the platform EventBridge bus without ever blocking on the work. From the bus, a dispatch Lambda runs synchronously while an EventBridge rule load-levels long-running agent-task events into an SQS work queue with a dead-letter queue for poison tasks; queue depth is the scaling signal a future worker fleet consumes. Compute runs on an EC2 Spot instance in a VPC public subnet with no NAT gateway, launched from a custom AMI, behind a security group whose egress is an allow-list; the instance reaches S3 over a free gateway endpoint and the internet through an internet gateway, and operators connect only through SSM Session Manager. The Go integration layers run the platform's own inference behind one provider abstraction with two implementations, Ollama local and Amazon Bedrock managed, and a router that is itself a provider and chooses between them per request under a constraint that a local-only prompt may never leave the network; a bounded loop controller pursues goals, and a tool loop lets the model trigger an n8n workflow or submit an OpenClaw agent task, but a conversation in which a write tool has run is never retried. Amazon Bedrock sits inside the region but outside the VPC and is the one path where the prompt leaves the network, billed per token. A multi-region CloudTrail with its own KMS key records the account, CloudWatch carries dashboards and alarms, and AWS Budgets with Cost Anomaly Detection guard the bill; the three send to three separate SNS topics for three audiences. n8n, OpenClaw and Ollama are deployed by their own repositories, not this one. The extension model of Milestone 17 is architecture only and adds no AWS resource.
svg.text: Thirteen CloudFormation stacks + an image pipeline · a non-blocking webhook front door, a router, a work queue, and the monitoring, security and cost planes
Event-driven front door — it verifies, filters, and publishes; it never blocks on the work
Spot interruption plane — the account watches
Work queue — decouple long-running tasks from orchestration
Security group — no inbound · egress ALLOW-LIST (M14)
Custom AMI — boots in 2.5 s (was 76 s)
spot-drain agent — saves work to S3 on notice
Docker · Go · Node · Python · CloudWatch agent · Ollama

g.text: Amazon Bedrock (M8) — MANAGED, outside the VPC

svg.text: artifacts · drained work → S3 (via the gateway endpoint)
SigV4 · instance role · NO stored credential
GO INTEGRATION LAYERS (this repository) — the contracts, not the servers. arrow points INWARD (M17): a core owns each interface, a client implements it, one factory knows the catalogue

g.text: M5 · M6 · the tools the model may call
a client + one factory line + a test

svg.text: COMPONENT REPOSITORIES — they own the deployments; this repository owns only the contracts (M5, M6, M7) and creates no AWS resources for any of them

g.text: What happens, in what order. Idempotency key · payload sanitised.
Concurrency-tolerant workflows feed the work queue (M16).
An errand: tools, a loop, a budget. It calls its OWN model.
Its OUTPUT is untrusted: a credential in it is rejected, not published.
LOCAL — the prompt never leaves the network.
LLM_PROVIDER=ollama — the DEFAULT; the router prefers it.

svg.text: What each milestone added — and what is still ahead

g.text: CloudFormation — VPC · IAM · EC2 · S3 · EventBridge · CloudWatch — the instance is disposable
EC2 Spot + interruption handling — disposability is safe
Custom AMIs — disposability is cheap (76 s → 2.5 s boot)
OpenClaw — it can delegate work, with a budget it cannot exceed
Ollama + provider abstraction — it can think, locally
Amazon Bedrock — the same interface, hosted
Claude — the model can act (“a retry is safe here” is withdrawn)
Hybrid routing — the router is a provider; choose per request
Loop engineering — pursue a goal, bounded and recoverable
GitHub webhooks — an event-driven front door that never blocks
Observability — dashboards, EMF metrics, alarms, health probes
Security — egress allow-list, CloudTrail + KMS, CIS-style alarms
Cost — Budgets, Cost Anomaly Detection, a billing alarm; arm64 · S3 IT
Scalability — an SQS work queue; shaped to scale horizontally
Future Extensions — a test-enforced extension model (architecture only)
Designed, not built — the next milestones
MCP — client + server on the existing tool-runner seam (next)
RAG / vector store — a memory.Store interface is specified, not shipped
Worker fleet + Auto Scaling group — drains the queue; the signal exists
Multi-tenancy · GPU inference on our own hardware — not built

svg.text: This is a redrawn snapshot at Milestone 17. The Mermaid view in current-architecture.md is the version kept current each milestone.
